seoTitle: How to Set Working Hours and Office Time in Slack
metaDescription: Set a custom notification schedule in Slack to silence alerts outside your working hours and protect your personal downtime.
intro: Protecting your downtime while staying available for coworkers means setting clear notification boundaries in Slack. This guide covers configuring a custom weekly schedule so alerts pause automatically outside your set hours.

steps.heading: Open your preferences
steps.body: Click your profile avatar in the lower left corner and select Preferences.

steps.heading: Go to the Notifications tab
steps.body: Select Notifications from the sidebar to access your global alert settings.

steps.heading: Find the notification schedule
steps.body: Scroll down to the dedicated Notification Schedule section.

steps.heading: Set your days and hours
steps.body: Choose weekdays or custom days from the frequency dropdown, then set your exact start and end times.

steps.heading: Verify your time zone
steps.body: Check Language & Region settings to confirm your local time zone is accurate for coworkers in other regions.

faq.question: Will Slack still deliver messages outside my working hours?
faq.answer: Messages still arrive, but notifications are silenced until your scheduled hours resume.

faq.question: Does this affect direct messages differently than channel mentions?
faq.answer: The schedule generally applies to all notification types unless you've customized specific channel overrides.

faq.question: Can I set different hours for different days?
faq.answer: Slack's schedule primarily supports a consistent daily window rather than fully independent settings per day.

faq.question: How do coworkers see my working hours?
faq.answer: Your presence indicator often reflects a paused or away state outside your set schedule, giving teammates a visual cue.
